sections.title: Releases
sections.subtitle: Eight notable releases, led by month-specific anchors where evidence supports them.

items.title: New Super Mario Bros.
items.body: A side-scrolling Mario returns as handheld comfort food.
items.sourceNote: Regional timing varies around May/June 2006.

items.title: The Elder Scrolls IV: Oblivion
items.body: Bethesda makes the new generation feel like a landscape you can walk into.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for March 2006.

items.title: Kingdom Hearts II
items.body: Square and Disney's sequel carries PS2 players through the new-console noise.
items.sourceNote: Regional timing varies; Europe follows later in 2006.

items.title: Tomb Raider: Legend
items.body: Lara Croft returns in a cleaner, more cinematic form.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for April 2006.

items.title: Okami
items.body: Clover turns brushwork into one of the PS2's most beautiful late works.
items.sourceNote: Initial release is widely placed in 2006; Europe follows in 2007.

items.title: Bully
items.body: Rockstar takes open-world mischief back to school.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for October 2006.

items.title: Gears of War
items.body: Epic gives the 360 its first defining cover-shooter monster.
items.sourceNote: Widely listed for November 2006.

items.title: The Legend of Zelda: Twilight Princess
items.body: Nintendo bridges GameCube twilight and Wii dawn with one Hyrule.
items.sourceNote: Regional timing varies; Europe receives Wii version at launch in December.

sections.title: Hardware
sections.subtitle: Platform, buying and industry context for the month and its wider year.

items.title: Wii launches in Europe
items.body: Nintendo's motion-control console reaches Europe on December 8 and immediately turns spectators into players.
items.sourceNote: Wii references list December 8, 2006 for Europe.

items.title: PS3 misses Europe in 2006
items.body: Sony's console launches in Japan and North America, but Europe waits until March 2007.
items.sourceNote: GamesIndustry.biz covers the European PS3 delay.

items.title: Xbox 360 finds its identity
items.body: Gears of War, Xbox Live and high-definition marketing help Microsoft's console feel established.
items.sourceNote: 2006 platform context; verify individual UK sales claims separately.

items.title: DS Lite strengthens Nintendo handheld momentum
items.body: The lighter, brighter DS redesign helps make touch-screen play fashionable.
items.sourceNote: DS Lite references list 2006 regional launches.

sections.title: Online Life
sections.subtitle: How the network felt around the edges of play.

items.title: Services and servers
items.body: Online life means Xbox Live friends lists, PSN's beginning, Wii friend codes, forums, GameFAQs and videos that load just slowly enough to feel precious.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Friends and parties
items.body: Gears of War makes Xbox Live co-op and competitive play feel like a reason to own the box.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Downloads and stores
items.body: DS Wi-Fi and Animal Crossing/Mario Kart habits keep handheld online slightly awkward but charming.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

items.title: Everyday connection
items.body: Broadband is increasingly normal in UK gaming households, but downloads are still planned rather than invisible.
items.sourceNote: Online-life context is synthesised from platform history, period reporting and UK access patterns; verify with period web/magazine sources before final publication.

sections.title: What It Felt Like
sections.subtitle: A curator's narrative sketch of the month as lived culture.

items.title: The room
items.body: It felt like the future had three personalities: Xbox 360's expensive gloss, Wii's living-room magic trick and PS3's delayed promise.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The shelf
items.body: The Wii was less a normal console than something you demonstrated to relatives.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The conversation
items.body: For UK players, Sony's absence in Christmas 2006 gave the season a strange shape: Nintendo had surprise, Microsoft had momentum, PS2 still had comfort.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.

items.title: The afterimage
items.body: Games were becoming more social in the room and more connected through the wire, but neither change felt settled yet.
items.sourceNote: Interpretive narrative based on researched anchors; keep labelled as atmosphere rather than fact.
